elements.label: A first remote terminal receives an address and event data and uses a database to correlate the address to location-based data.
A second terminal includes a map display and receives location-based data from a location system based on the second terminal's own location.

Maps JavaScript API map display marker
location bias nearby search Places API

elements.label: The second terminal transmits its location-based data to the first terminal over a communication channel.
search_queries: Maps SDK for Android marker location
effective_queries: Maps SDK for Android marker location

elements.label: The first terminal stores the second terminal's location-based data in memory.
When the stored location-based data and event data satisfy specified criteria, the first terminal sends the correlated location-based data and event data to the second terminal.
search_queries: Places API nearby search location bias
Maps JavaScript API route origin destination
effective_queries: Places API nearby search location bias
Maps JavaScript API route origin destination

elements.label: The second terminal automatically inputs the received location-based data into the display unit to show a map that includes the first address.
